sources.population: Pakistan Bureau of Statistics, Census 2023. Karachi Division total population 20.3 million.
sources.sex_ratio: CIA World Factbook 2024. Pakistan 65+: 0.87 male/female = 53.5% female. Total population: 1.04 male/female = 49.0% female. Karachi urban estimate applied.
sources.ethnicity: Pakistan Bureau of Statistics, Census 2024. Ethnic groups in Karachi: Urdu-speaking (Muhajir) 50.6%, Pashtun 13.5%, Sindhi 11.1%, Punjabi 10.7%, Balochi 4.0%, other 10.1%. Source: Wikipedia, Ethnic groups in Karachi, citing 2024 Pakistani census.
sources.age_distribution: UN World Population Prospects 2024. Pakistan median age 20.8yr. ICU cohort age bands set per Promise Abegunde spec: 55% 65+, 45% 18-64 — not population-representative.
sources.infection_source_notes: Pneumonia weighted higher than US profiles: Karachi urban air pollution burden (WHO Global Ambient Air Quality Database 2024), TB co-infection prevalence (Pakistan NTP Annual Report 2023), consistent with Rhee C et al. JAMA Intern Med 2017 base distribution adjusted for LMIC urban context.

ethnicity_label_note: Engine maps Karachi population to 'asian' ethnicity bucket. Internal label only — does not distinguish Urdu-speaking, Pashtun, Sindhi, Punjabi, Balochi subgroups. Subgroup breakdown documented in profile sources above.
age_band_source: Promise Abegunde cohort specification. Not population-representative of Karachi general population.

infection_source_modifier.note: Pneumonia boosted +6% vs US default to reflect Karachi urban air pollution and TB burden. UTI reduced -3% to compensate. Source: WHO GAAQD 2024, Pakistan NTP 2023.

known_limitations: Engine ethnicity schema does not support intra-Asian subgroup distinctions (Urdu/Pashtun/Sindhi/Punjabi/Balochi). All mapped to 'asian'.
Sex ratio applied from national Pakistan data — Karachi-specific sex ratio by age not available in public datasets.
ICU age band (55% 65+) per Promise spec, not Karachi population pyramid (Pakistan median age 20.8yr means far fewer elderly in general population).
Sepsis infection source distribution adapted from US Rhee 2017 base with urban Pakistan adjustments — no Karachi-specific ICU sepsis source distribution available in published literature.
Afebrile and AMS-only rates driven by age/comorbidity logic, not Pakistan-specific clinical data.

continuous_vars._source: IDF Diabetes Atlas 2021; WHO STEPS Pakistan 2014-2018; NHANES reference for lab norms; Pakistan NCD risk factor survey.

hba1c.source: IDF Diabetes Atlas 2021; Pakistan DM prevalence ~26.7% adults (IDF 2021); elevated urban Karachi burden

bmi.source: WHO STEPS Pakistan 2014-2018; lower mean BMI vs US despite high DM; overweight threshold lower in South Asian populations

systolic_bp.source: WHO Pakistan NCD risk factor survey; HTN prevalence ~40-45% Pakistan adults (Kearney 2005 projections)

creatinine.source: NHANES reference adjusted for LMIC higher CKD burden; DM+HTN driven CKD; KDIGO 2022

egfr.source: CKD-EPI 2021; elevated CKD burden due to high DM+HTN prevalence in urban Pakistan

temporal_trends._source: IDF Diabetes Atlas 2021-2030 projections; WHO EMRO NCD trend data; Pakistan NTP 2023

disease_prevalence_shift._unit: absolute prevalence change (fraction) at 6-month drift interval

prescription_shift._unit: relative change fraction at 6-month drift interval

6.source: IDF Atlas 2021; WHO EMRO NCD trends; Pakistan NTP 2023

disease_prevalence_shift._unit: absolute prevalence change (fraction) at 12-month drift interval

prescription_shift._unit: relative change fraction at 12-month drift interval

12.source: IDF Atlas 2021; WHO EMRO NCD trends; Pakistan NTP 2023

disease_prevalence_shift._unit: absolute prevalence change (fraction) at 18-month drift interval

prescription_shift._unit: relative change fraction at 18-month drift interval

18.source: IDF Atlas 2021; WHO EMRO NCD trends; Pakistan NTP 2023
